arguments.content:
# Swarm Notifications — Bedivere (Documentation)

## What this is

When a swarm pipeline run finishes — whether it succeeds, fails, or times out — the **notification system** can alert you through configurable channels. You choose which events trigger a notification and where the notification goes.

Notifications are **opt-in**. If you do not add a `notify` section to your swarm YAML, nothing fires and there is zero overhead.

## Where to configure

Notifications live under a top-level `notify` key in your `loop.yaml` (or any swarm pipeline YAML), at the same level as `agents` and `reviewers`:

```yaml
swarm:
  name: release-checks
  workspace: .
  mode: loop

  notify:
    on_success: true
    on_failure: true
    on_timeout: false
    channels:
      - type: webhook
        url: ${OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL}
        label: team-slack
      - type: file
        path: .omp/runs/{{name}}-{{timestamp}}.log
```

## Events

Three lifecycle events control when notifications fire:

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `on_success` | boolean | `false` | Fire when the pipeline completes with zero errors |
| `on_failure` | boolean | `false` | Fire when the pipeline completes with errors or a fatal exception |
| `on_timeout` | boolean | `false` | Fire when the pipeline is aborted (timeout, Ctrl+C, signal) |

All three default to `false`. Set any combination to `true` — only matching events dispatch.

## Channel types

### Webhook (`type: webhook`)

Sends an HTTP request to a URL with a JSON payload describing the pipeline result.

```yaml
channels:
  - type: webhook
    url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/T00/B00/xxx
    method: POST          # "POST" (default) or "PUT"
    label: team-slack     # optional — used in logs
    headers:
      Content-Type: application/json
      Authorization: "Bearer ${OMP_NOTIFY_ALERT_TOKEN}"
    retry:
      max_attempts: 3     # default: 1 (no retry)
      backoff_ms: 1000    # default: 1000
```

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | string | `OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL` env var | HTTP endpoint. Supports `${VAR}` env-var expressions. |
| `method` | `"POST"` \| `"PUT"` | `"POST"` | HTTP method |
| `headers` | record | `{}` | Extra HTTP headers. Values resolve `${VAR}` expressions. |
| `retry.max_attempts` | number | `1` | Total delivery attempts including the first |
| `retry.backoff_ms` | number | `1000` | Delay between retries in milliseconds |
| `label` | string | URL hostname | Human-readable name for log output |

If `url` is omitted or resolves to empty, the channel is **skipped** with a warning — not a hard error.

#### Webhook payload

A `POST` to the configured URL sends this JSON body:

```json
{
  "pipeline": "release-checks",
  "workspace": "/home/user/project",
  "status": "completed",
  "iterations": 3,
  "agent_count": 5,
  "errors": [],
  "duration_ms": 45230,
  "timestamp": "2026-07-14T10:30:00.000Z",
  "mode": "loop",
  "summary": "Pipeline release-checks completed: 3 iterations, 5 agents, 0 errors"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pipeline` | string | Swarm name from the YAML |
| `workspace` | string | Absolute workspace path |
| `status` | `"completed"` \| `"failed"` \| `"aborted"` | Pipeline outcome |
| `iterations` | number | Iterations completed (0 if fatal error) |
| `agent_count` | number | Total agents in the swarm definition |
| `errors` | string[] | Error messages (empty on success) |
| `duration_ms` | number | Wall-clock duration of the pipeline run |
| `timestamp` | ISO 8601 | When the notification was dispatched |
| `mode` | string | Pipeline mode (`loop`, `pipeline`, `parallel`, `sequential`) |
| `summary` | string | Human-readable one-line summary |

### File (`type: file`)

Appends a one-line summary to a file on disk.

```yaml
channels:
  - type: file
    path: .omp/runs/{{name}}-{{timestamp}}.log
    mode: append           # "append" (default) or "overwrite"
    label: run-log         # optional — used in logs
```

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `path` | string | `OMP_NOTIFY_FILE_PATH` env var | File path relative to workspace. Supports template variables. |
| `mode` | `"append"` \| `"overwrite"` | `"append"` | Append to existing file or replace it |
| `label` | string | basename of path | Human-readable name for log output |

If `path` is omitted or resolves to empty, the channel is **skipped** with a warning.

#### Template variables

File paths support `{{name}}`, `{{timestamp}}`, and `{{status}}`:

| Variable | Expands to | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `{{name}}` | Swarm pipeline name | `release-checks` |
| `{{timestamp}}` | ISO 8601 timestamp (filesystem-safe) | `2026-07-14T10-30-00` |
| `{{status}}` | Pipeline result status | `completed`, `failed`, `aborted` |

Each notification appends one line to the file:

```
[2026-07-14T10:30:00.000Z] release-checks completed: 3 iterations, 5 agents, 0 errors (45.2s)
```

## Environment variables

Sensitive values — webhook URLs with embedded tokens, API keys — should **never** be committed to YAML. Use environment variables instead.

### Channel-level env vars

Values in the YAML that contain `${VAR}` are resolved from the environment at dispatch time:

```yaml
url: ${OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL}              # → process.env.OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL
headers:
  Authorization: "Bearer ${OMP_NOTIFY_TOKEN}" # → process.env.OMP_NOTIFY_TOKEN
```

### Fallback env vars

If a required field is absent from YAML, the system checks a channel-type fallback:

| Channel | Field | Fallback env var |
|---|---|---|
| `webhook` | `url` | `OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL` |
| `file` | `path` | `OMP_NOTIFY_FILE_PATH` |

This lets you set a machine-wide default without touching any YAML file:

```bash
export OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL="https://hooks.slack.com/services/T00/B00/xxx"
```

### Resolution order

1. Explicit YAML value (after `${VAR}` expansion)
2. Channel-type fallback env var
3. Undefined → channel skipped with a warning

## Retry behavior

Webhook channels can be configured with automatic retries:

```yaml
retry:
  max_attempts: 3
  backoff_ms: 1000
```

- `max_attempts: 1` (the default) means no retry — one attempt, then give up.
- Each retry waits `backoff_ms` between attempts.
- A channel that exhausts all retries is logged as a warning; other channels are unaffected.
- File channels do not retry (disk writes are synchronous and either succeed or fail immediately).

## Complete example

```yaml
# release.yaml — swarm pipeline with notifications
swarm:
  name: release-checks
  workspace: .
  mode: loop
  target_count: 3

  # Agents and reviewers…
  agents:
    builder:
      role: Build and test the release artifact
      task: Run the full test suite and build
    reviewer:
      role: Review the build output
      task: Check for regressions

  reviewers:
    core: [atropos, lachesis, clotho]

  # Notification configuration
  notify:
    on_success: true
    on_failure: true
    on_timeout: false

    channels:
      # Slack notification via webhook
      - type: webhook
        url: ${OMP_SLACK_WEBHOOK}
        label: team-slack
        retry:
          max_attempts: 3
          backoff_ms: 2000

      # Internal alerting system with auth
      - type: webhook
        url: https://alerts.internal.example.com/hooks/pipeline
        method: POST
        headers:
          Authorization: "Bearer ${OMP_ALERT_TOKEN}"
          Content-Type: application/json
        label: internal-alerts

      # Local run log
      - type: file
        path: .omp/runs/{{name}}-{{timestamp}}.log
        label: run-log
```

## How it works

```text
pipeline completes
       │
       ▼
buildSummaryMessage()   — produces summary string
       │
       ▼
notifyRouter.dispatch() — checks on_success / on_failure / on_timeout
       │                  against PipelineResult.status
       ▼
fan-out to channels     — each channel dispatched independently
       │                  (non-blocking, fire-and-forget)
       ▼
channel success / fail  — logged; one channel's failure never
                          blocks another or the pipeline
```

Dispatch is **non-blocking**: the pipeline reports completion to the user immediately, and notifications are sent asynchronously in the background. If a webhook hangs, it times out and is retried per its retry policy; other channels proceed unaffected.

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Check |
|---|---|
| No notifications fire | Are `on_success` / `on_failure` / `on_timeout` set to `true` for your event? |
| Webhook channel skipped | Does `url` resolve to a non-empty string? Check the env var. |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Is the `Authorization` header set correctly? Did the token expire? |
| File not created | Is the parent directory writable? The file path is relative to the swarm workspace. |
| Duplicate notifications | Each configured channel fires independently. Remove duplicates from `channels:`. |

## Extensibility

The channel system uses a discriminated union on `type`. Adding a new channel type (e.g. `slack`, `email`, `discord`) is a new variant — existing configs are unaffected. Unknown `type` values are skipped with a warning. See the implementation notes in `bors.md` for the `normalizeChannel()` extension point.

{"content": "# Swarm Notifications — Bedivere (Documentation)

## What this is

When a swarm pipeline run finishes — whether it succeeds, fails, or times out — the **notification system** can alert you through configurable channels. You choose which events trigger a notification and where the notification goes.

Notifications are **opt-in**. If you do not add a `notify` section to your swarm YAML, nothing fires and there is zero overhead.

## Where to configure

Notifications live under a top-level `notify` key in your `loop.yaml` (or any swarm pipeline YAML), at the same level as `agents` and `reviewers`:

```yaml
swarm:
  name: release-checks
  workspace: .
  mode: loop

  notify:
    on_success: true
    on_failure: true
    on_timeout: false
    channels:
      - type: webhook
        url: ${OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL}
        label: team-slack
      - type: file
        path: .omp/runs/{{name}}-{{timestamp}}.log
```

## Events

Three lifecycle events control when notifications fire:

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `on_success` | boolean | `false` | Fire when the pipeline completes with zero errors |
| `on_failure` | boolean | `false` | Fire when the pipeline completes with errors or a fatal exception |
| `on_timeout` | boolean | `false` | Fire when the pipeline is aborted (timeout, Ctrl+C, signal) |

All three default to `false`. Set any combination to `true` — only matching events dispatch.

## Channel types

### Webhook (`type: webhook`)

Sends an HTTP request to a URL with a JSON payload describing the pipeline result.

```yaml
channels:
  - type: webhook
    url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/T00/B00/xxx
    method: POST          # "POST" (default) or "PUT"
    label: team-slack     # optional — used in logs
    headers:
      Content-Type: application/json
      Authorization: "Bearer ${OMP_NOTIFY_ALERT_TOKEN}"
    retry:
      max_attempts: 3     # default: 1 (no retry)
      backoff_ms: 1000    # default: 1000
```

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | string | `OMP_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL` env var | HTTP endpoint. Supports `${VAR}` env-var expressions. |
| `method` | `"POST"` \| `"PUT"` | `"POST"` | HTTP method |
| `headers` | record | `{}` | Extra HTTP headers. Values resolve `${VAR}` expressions. |
| `retry.max_attempts` | number | `1` | Total delivery attempts including the first |
| `retry.backoff_ms` | number | `1000` | Delay between retries in milliseconds |
| `label` | string | URL hostname | Human-readable name for log output |

If `url` is omitted or resolves to empty, the channel is **skipped** with a warning — not a hard error.

#### Webhook payload

A `POST` to the configured URL sends this JSON body:

```json
{
  "pipeline": "release-checks",
  "workspace": "/home/user/project",
  "status": "completed",
  "iterations": 3,
  "agent_count": 5,
  "errors": [],
  "duration_ms": 45230,
  "timestamp": "2026-07-14T10:30:00.000Z",
  "mode": "loop",
  "summary": "Pipeline release-checks completed: 3 iterations, 5 agents, 0 errors"
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pipeline` | string | Swarm name from the YAML |
| `workspace` | string | Absolute workspace path |
| `status` | `"completed"` \| `"failed"` \| `"aborted"` | Pipeline outcome |
| `iterations` | number | Iterations completed (0 if fatal error) |
| `agent_count` | number | Total agents in the swarm definition |
| `errors` | string[] | Error messages (empty on success) |
| `duration_ms` | number | Wall-clock duration of the pipeline run |
| `timestamp` | ISO 8601 | When the notification was dispatched |
| `mode` | string | Pipeline mode (`loop`, `pipeline`, `parallel`, `sequential`) |
| `summary` | string | Human-readable one-line summary |

### File (`type: file`)

Appends a one-line summary to a file on disk.

```yaml
channels:
  - type: file
    path: .omp/runs/{{name}}-{{timestamp}}.log
    mode: append           # "append" (default) or "overwrite"
    label: run-log         # optional — used in logs
```

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `path` | string | `OMP_NOTIFY_FILE_PATH` env var | File path relative to workspace. Supports template variables. |
| `mode` | `"append"` \| `"overwrite"` | `"append"` | Append to existing file or replace it |
| `label` | string | basename of path | Human-readable name for log output |

If `path` is omitted or resolves to empty, the channel is **skipped** with a warning.

#### Template variables

File paths support `{{name}}`, `{{timestamp}}`, and `{{status}}`:

| Variable | Expands to | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `{{name}}` | Swarm pipeline name | `release-checks` |
| `{{timestamp}}` | ISO 8601 timestamp (filesystem-safe) | `2026-07-14T10-30-00` |
| `{{status}}` | Pipeline result status | `completed`, `failed`, `aborted` |

Each notification appends one line to the file:

```
[2026-07-14T10:30:00.000Z] release-checks completed: 3 iterations, 5 agents, 0 errors (45.2s)
```

## Environment variables

### Channel-level env vars

```yaml
  Authorization: "Bearer ${OMP_NOTIFY_TOKEN}" # → process.env.OMP_NOTIFY_TOKEN
```

1. Explicit YAML value (after `${VAR}` expansion)
2. Channel-type fallback env var
3. Undefined → channel skipped with a warning


# Accessibility

`omp` is a terminal application. Accessibility in the terminal has different constraints than the web: no DOM, no ARIA, no browser zoom. This document covers what `omp` provides and how to configure it for screen readers, color-blindness, low vision, and keyboard-only use.

## Quick-start: screen reader users

```bash
omp config set symbolPreset ascii
omp config set statusLine.preset ascii
omp config set statusLine.separator ascii
```

These three settings replace every Unicode glyph, Nerd Font icon, and Powerline separator with plain ASCII text that screen readers can speak. The result reads as labelled text instead of silent or garbled Unicode.

## Symbol presets

`omp` renders status indicators, tool icons, tree connectors, box-drawing borders, and language badges with a configurable glyph set. Three presets exist:

| Preset | Description | Screen reader |
|---|---|---|
| `unicode` | Standard Unicode symbols (default) | Partial — some symbols speak, others are silent |
| `nerd` | Nerd Font private-use codepoints | No — private-use area is never spoken |
| `ascii` | Plain ASCII with text labels | Full — every element has a readable label |

| `nerd` | Nerd Font private-use codepoints | No — private-use area is never spoken |
| `ascii` | Plain ASCII with text labels | Full — every element has a readable label |
